metadata.name: NetBird Reverse Proxy Service
metadata.description: Creates a NetBird reverse proxy service with one backend target and configurable authentication.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Creates a NetBird reverse proxy service with one backend target.

## Prerequisites
- Create an admin PAT for the NetBird management API before rendering `provider.tf`.
- If `target_type=peer`, provide `target_peer_name`. The template looks up the peer in `main.tf` with `data "netbird_peer"` and uses its ID automatically.
- If `target_type=host`, `domain`, or `subnet`, provide `target_network_name` plus the matching resource name field for the selected type. The template looks up the NetBird network with `data "netbird_network"`, then resolves the backend resource with `data "netbird_network_resource"` and uses that ID automatically.
- For non-peer targets, the final reverse proxy `target_type` is inferred from the resolved network resource address to avoid host/domain/subnet mismatches during apply.
- If the resolved target is a subnet, NetBird requires a backend host IP within that CIDR range. When `target_host` is empty, the template defaults it to the network resource address without the CIDR suffix.
- If you choose `custom` domain mode, provide the validated domain name. The template resolves it with `data "netbird_reverse_proxy_domain"` before attaching it to the service.
- If you use bearer auth distribution groups, provide `bearer_distribution_group_names` as a comma-separated list of existing NetBird group names. The template resolves them with `data "netbird_group"`.

## Apply
- Render `provider.tf` and `main.tf`.
- Review the selected domain mode, target lookup inputs, and authentication method.
- Run `terraform fmt`, `terraform plan`, and `terraform apply`.

## Notes
- This template renders one item inside the required `targets = [...]` list. If you need multiple backends, duplicate that object in the rendered `main.tf`.
- `auth_mode=none` renders an empty `auth = {}` object for a public service.

## References
- [Reverse Proxy Authentication](https://docs.netbird.io/manage/reverse-proxy/authentication)
- [Terraform Resource](https://github.com/netbirdio/terraform-provider-netbird/blob/main/docs/resources/reverse_proxy_service.md)
- [Reverse Proxy Domain Data Source](https://github.com/netbirdio/terraform-provider-netbird/blob/main/docs/data-sources/reverse_proxy_domain.md)
- [Peer Data Source](https://github.com/netbirdio/terraform-provider-netbird/blob/main/docs/data-sources/peer.md)
- [Network Data Source](https://github.com/netbirdio/terraform-provider-netbird/blob/main/docs/data-sources/network.md)
- [Network Resource Data Source](https://github.com/netbirdio/terraform-provider-netbird/blob/main/docs/data-sources/network_resource.md)
- [Group Data Source](https://github.com/netbirdio/terraform-provider-netbird/blob/main/docs/data-sources/group.md)

variables.name: service_name
variables.title: Service Name
variables.description: Name of the reverse proxy service.

variables.name: domain_mode
variables.title: Domain Mode
variables.description: Use a free NetBird reverse proxy domain or a prevalidated custom domain.

variables.name: custom_domain
variables.title: Custom Domain
variables.description: Validated custom domain for the reverse proxy service.

variables.name: service_enabled
variables.title: Enabled
variables.description: Enable the service after creation.

variables.name: pass_host_header
variables.title: Pass Host Header
variables.description: Pass the original client Host header through to the backend.

variables.name: rewrite_redirects
variables.title: Rewrite Redirects
variables.description: Rewrite backend redirect locations to the public-facing service domain.

variables.name: target_type
variables.title: Target Type
variables.description: Target type for the backend service.

variables.name: target_peer_name
variables.title: Peer Name
variables.description: Existing NetBird peer name to resolve via `data "netbird_peer"` when `target_type=peer`.

variables.name: target_network_name
variables.title: Network Name
variables.description: Existing NetBird network name that contains the selected host, domain, or subnet resource.

variables.name: target_host_name
variables.title: Host Resource Name
variables.description: Existing NetBird network resource name for a host target.

variables.name: target_domain_name
variables.title: Domain Resource Name
variables.description: Existing NetBird network resource name for a domain target.

variables.name: target_subnet_name
variables.title: Subnet Resource Name
variables.description: Existing NetBird network resource name for a subnet target.

variables.name: target_port
variables.title: Target Port
variables.description: Backend port for the target.

variables.name: target_protocol
variables.title: Target Protocol
variables.description: Protocol used between the reverse proxy and the backend.

variables.name: target_enabled
variables.title: Target Enabled
variables.description: Enable the backend target after creation.

variables.name: target_host
variables.title: Resolved Host
variables.description: Optional backend IP or domain override for the target. Subnet targets need a host IP within the CIDR range.

variables.name: target_path
variables.title: Path Prefix
variables.description: Optional URL path prefix for the backend target.

variables.name: auth_mode
variables.title: Authentication Mode
variables.description: Authentication method exposed by the reverse proxy service.

variables.name: auth_password
variables.title: Password
variables.description: Shared password for password-based access.

variables.name: auth_pin
variables.title: PIN
variables.description: Shared PIN for PIN-based access.

variables.name: bearer_distribution_group_names
variables.title: Bearer Distribution Groups
variables.description: Comma-separated existing NetBird group names allowed for bearer-auth access.

variables.name: netbird_management_url
variables.title: Management URL
variables.description: NetBird Management API URL. Trailing slashes are trimmed. TLS certificates must be trusted by the OpenTofu runner because the provider does not expose a skip-verification option.

variables.name: netbird_pat
variables.title: Admin PAT
variables.description: NetBird admin personal access token (PAT).

variables.name: netbird_tenant_account
variables.title: Tenant Account
variables.description: Optional tenant account ID for impersonation.
